name: serialized-data-interface

metadata.name: serialized-data-interface
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Serialized Data Interface for Juju Operators

documentation:
# Serialized Data Interface Library

https://pypi.org/project/serialized-data-interface/

This libraries enables its user to create serialized and validated Juju Operator interfaces.

An interface Schema will be defined through YAML e.g:

```yaml
v1:
  provides:
    type: object
    properties:
      access-key:
        type: string
      namespace:
        type: ['string', 'null']
      port:
        type: number
      secret-key:
        type: string
      secure:
        type: boolean
      service:
        type: string
    required:
      - access-key
      - port
      - secret-key
      - secure
      - service
```

When our charms interchange data, this library will validate the data through the schema on both
ends.

# Usage

In our charm metadata we would add the following lines to define our schema and the supported
versions:

```yaml
provides:
  oidc-client:
    interface: oidc-client
    schema: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/canonical/operator-schemas/oidc-schemas/oidc-client.yaml
    versions: [v1]
```

In this case SDI will pull the schema from Github during deployment. If we want to deploy our charm
in environments where Github isn't available we can pull the schemas during our build process by
adding some lines like this in our `tox.ini` file:

```
[testenv:build]
commands =
    charmcraft build
    python3 -m serialized_data_interface.local_sdi
```

# Real World Example

- Minio with Provider Interface
  - https://github.com/canonical/minio-operator/
- Argo Controller with Requirer Interface:
  - https://github.com/canonical/argo-operators/

# TODO

- Currently only provides data to App relations, should also support unit relations.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.events.RelationAvailableEvent
documentation: Event triggered when a relation is ready for requests.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.events.RelationFailedEvent
documentation: Event triggered when something went wrong with a relation.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.events.RelationReadyEvent
documentation: Event triggered when a remote relation has the expected data.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.events.RelationBrokenEvent
documentation: Event triggered when a remote relation has the expected data.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.events.EndpointWrapperEvents
documentation: Container for events for EndpointWrapper.

modules.name: serialized_data_interface.errors
documentation: Copyright 2023 Canonical Ltd.
See LICENSE file for licensing details.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.SDIException
documentation: Base exception for exceptions from this library.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.InvalidRoleError

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.InvalidRoleError.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the exception.

Args:
    role: The given role which was invalid.

The specified role is not one of "provides", "requires", or "peer".

The following property is available:

    * role: The role which was invalid.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.InvalidAppNameError
documentation:
The send_data method was called with an invalid app name.

Attributes:
    app_name: Name of the app which is invalid.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.AppNameOmittedError
documentation: The send_data method was called without an app name but versions don't match.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.UnknownEndpointError
documentation: The given relation endpoint name is was not found in metadata.yaml.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.SchemaError
documentation: Base class for errors with the schema.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.SchemaParseError
documentation: There was an error parsing the schemas YAML.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.InvalidSchemaError
documentation:
An invalid schema (not a valid JSONSchema) was found in the schema doc.

Attributes:
    version: The version of the schema which was invalid.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.InvalidSchemaVersionError
documentation:
An invalid version (not an int nor "vX" string) was found in the schema doc.

Attributes:
    version: The schema version which was invalid.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.RelationException
documentation: Base exception for relation exceptions from this library.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.UnversionedRelation
documentation: The relation is not yet complete due to missing remote version info.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.RelationError
documentation: Base class for actual errors from this library.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.IncompatibleVersionsError
documentation:
The remote application does not support any common schema versions.

Attributes:
    relation: The relation which failed.
    local_versions: The set of versions supported by the local side.
    remote_versions: The set of versions supported by the remote side.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.RelationParseError
documentation:
An error was encountered parsing data from the relation.

Attributes:
    relation: The Relation which caused the exception.
    entity: The Application or Unit which caused the exception.
    key: The data key which caused the exception.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.RelationDataError
documentation:
An error was encountered validating data against the schema.

Attributes:
    relation: The Relation which caused the exception.
    entity: The Application or Unit which caused the exception.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.MissingSchemaError
documentation:
Data was found in a relation which didn't have a matching schema.

Attributes:
    relation: The Relation which caused the exception.
    role: The role of the schema which couldn't be found.
    entity: The Application or Unit which caused the exception.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.RelationPermissionError
documentation: An attempt to write data to a disallowed bucket.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.errors.NoSchemaDefined
documentation:
A schema was referenced but could not be found.

Attributes:
    version: The version which is missing a schema.
    role: The role which is missing a schema.

modules.name: serialized_data_interface.relation
documentation:
Base class for libraries which wrap a relation endpoint with a Python API.

This provides the base for implementing relation libraries which use SDI
for data validation and de/serialization but provide a Python API on top
of that for ease of use.
Copyright 2023 Canonical Ltd.
See LICENSE file for licensing details.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.__init__
documentation:
Constructor for EndpointWrapper.

Args:
    charm: The charm that is instantiating the library.
    endpoint: The name of the relation endpoint to bind to
        (defaults to the INTERFACE, with any underscores
        changed to dashes).

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.app
documentation: Shortcut to self.charm.app.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.unit
documentation: Shortcut to self.charm.unit.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper._validate_relation_meta
documentation: Validate that the relation is setup properly in the metadata.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.versions
documentation: Set of supported versions.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.relations
documentation: The list of Relation instances associated with this endpoint.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.get_status
documentation: Get the suggested status for the given Relation.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.is_available
documentation:
Checks whether the given relation, or any relation if not specified, is available.

A given relation is available if the version negotation has succeeded.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.is_ready
documentation:
Checks whether the given relation, or any relation if not specified, is ready.

A given relation is ready if the remote side has sent valid data.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.is_failed
documentation: Checks whether the given relation, or any relation if not specified, has an error.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.unwrap
documentation:
Deserialize and validate the data from the relation.

Deserialize and validate all available data from the relation. The returned
dictionary has the same keys as `relation.data` (i.e., the local and remote
Applications and Units).

If the current unit is not the leader, the data for the current Application
will always be an empty dict.  See: https://bugs.launchpad.net/juju/+bug/1958530

Can raise:
    * IncompatibleVersionsError
    * RelationParseError
    * RelationDataError

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.relation.EndpointWrapper.wrap
documentation:
Validate and serialize the data and put it into the relation.

Validates that the local app and / or unit data is valid against the schema,
and if so, serializes it and returns it.

Can raise:
    * ModelError (when setting app data and not leader)
    * UnversionedRelation (when relation not available)
    * RelationParseError (when relation not available)
    * IncompatibleVersionsError (when relation not available)
    * RelationDataError (when given data is invalid)

Example:
        self.wrap(relation, {self.app: {"foo": "bar"}})

Base class for schema-based operator relation libraries.

Class Attributes:
    ROLE: Relation role which this class implements.
    Must be set by subclass.

    INTERFACE: Interface protocol name.
    Must be set by subclass.

    SCHEMA: A pathlib.Path pointing to the schemas YAML file,
    or schemas data structure. Must be set by subclass.

    LIMIT: Limit, if any, for relation endpoint connections.

Attributes:
    auto_data: Data to be automatically passed to `wrap()` as
    soon as possible for any relation. Must work with any supported
    version which could be negotiated.

    ignored_fields: Set of field names from remote relation data which
    should be ignored as not part of the schema. The default is the supported
    versions field, and the fields automatically populated by Juju.

modules.name: serialized_data_interface.utils
documentation: Copyright 2023 Canonical Ltd.

functions.name: serialized_data_interface.utils.get_schema
documentation: Ensures schema is retrieved if necessary, then loads it.

functions.name: serialized_data_interface.utils._get_schema_response_from_remote
documentation:
Returns a schema response object from a remote location, observing proxy settings.

Raises for status if unsuccessful.

proxy settings for each of http, https, and no_proxy are inferred from environment variables
in the order:
    JUJU_HTTP(S)_PROXY/JUJU_NO_PROXY
    HTTP(S)_PROXY/NO_PROXY

Args:
    url (str): String url to access the schema

Returns:
    requests.Response: Schema as a Response object

functions.name: serialized_data_interface.utils._get_proxy_settings_from_env
documentation: Returns proxy settings dict inferred from environment.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.utils.ZipFileWithPermissions

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.utils.ZipFileWithPermissions.extract
documentation: Extract a single member, preserving file permissions.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.utils.ZipFileWithPermissions.extractall
documentation: Extract all content, preserving file permissions.
Wrapper around ZipFile which preserves file permissions.
Custom ZipFile class due to extractall not keeping file permissions
Official Python bug: https://bugs.python.org/issue15795

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.__init__
documentation: Initialize the mock provider / requirer.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.relation
documentation: The Relation instance, if created.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.relate
documentation:
Create a relation to the charm under test.

Starts the version negotiation, and returns the Relation instance.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.remote_context
documentation:
Temporarily change the context to the remote side of the relation.

The test runs within the context of the local charm under test.  This
means that the relation data on the remote side cannot be written, the
app and units references are from the local charm's perspective, etc.
This temporarily patches things to behave as if we were running on the
remote charm instead.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.add_unit
documentation: Add a unit to the relation.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.is_available
documentation: Same as EndpointWrapper.is_available, but with the remote context.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.is_ready
documentation: Same as EndpointWrapper.is_ready, but with the remote context.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.is_failed
documentation: Same as EndpointWrapper.is_failed, but with the remote context.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.wrap
documentation: Same as EndpointWrapper.wrap, but ensures the Harness is updated.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.testing.MockRemoteRelationMixin.unwrap
documentation: Same as EndpointWrapper.unwrap, but from the remote context.
Adds unit testing helpers to EndpointWrapper.

modules.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi
documentation: Copyright 2023 Canonical Ltd.

functions.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.get_interfaces
documentation:
Reads metadata.yaml to retrieve schema-checked interface objects.

The returned dictionary will always contain keys for each interface that
defines a schema, but the associated values may be None, if no relations
have been made yet on that interface. This is because instantiating the
SerializedDataInterface class requires agreeing on a schema version by
both sides of the relation.

functions.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.get_interface
documentation:
Reads metadata.yaml to retrieve schema-checked interface object.

If the interface has established relations, send_versions will be automatically called for
each of them.  If the interface has no established relations, will return None instead.

classes.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the SDI instance.

Args:
    charm: The charm that this is being used with.
    endpoint: The name of the relation endpoint to use.
    schemas: The mapping of versions to role-entity-schema mappings.
    versions: Set of supported versions.
    role: The role for the local side of the relation.
    ignored_fields: Optional set of fields to ignore when validating and deserializing.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.app
documentation: Shortcut to self.charm.app.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.unit
documentation: Shortcut to self.charm.unit.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.max_version
documentation: The maximum version supported by this instance.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.get_version
documentation:
Get the maximum compatible version for a given Relation.

Can raise:
    * UnversionedRelation
    * RelationParseError
    * IncompatibleVersionsError

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.send_versions
documentation:
Send the list of supported versions to the related app.

If not the leader, does nothing.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.get_data
documentation:
Get unwrapped data for all relations.

If any relation has an error or is unversioned, it will be surfaced immediately.

Returns a mapping of (relation, relation.app) tuples to the unwrapped data for that app.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.send_data
documentation:
Send data to related app(s).

If `app_name` is given, the data will only be sent to relations with that remote
application. Otherwise, it will be sent to all relations. Note that this means that
either the data given has to be compatible with any established relation versions.

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.unwrap
documentation:
Deserialize and validate the data from the relation.

Deserialize and validate all available data from the relation. The returned
dictionary has the same keys as `relation.data` (i.e., the local and remote
Applications and Units).

If the current unit is not the leader, the data for the current Application
will always be an empty dict.  See: https://bugs.launchpad.net/juju/+bug/1958530

Can raise:
    * IncompatibleVersionsError
    * RelationParseError
    * RelationDataError

methods.name: serialized_data_interface.sdi.SerializedDataInterface.wrap
documentation:
Validate and serialize the data and put it into the relation.

Validates that the local app and / or unit data is valid against the schema,
and if so, serializes it and returns it.

Can raise:
    * ModelError (when setting app data and not leader)
    * UnversionedRelation (when relation not available)
    * RelationParseError (when relation not available)
    * IncompatibleVersionsError (when relation not available)
    * RelationDataError (when given data is invalid)

Example:
        self.wrap(relation, {self.app: {"foo": "bar"}})

Represents a schema-defined interface between two charms.

The schema should match the JSON Schema specification, though
should be written in YAML.
